title: Combining equations
description: Practice combining equations to learn when and how variables can be eliminated in a system of linear equations.
topic_path: Solving systems of equations with elimination

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the result of adding these two equations?**

$\begin{align}
2x+3y &= -5  \\\\
5x-y &= -12
\end{align}$

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
When we $\maroonD{\text{add}}$ equations to each other, we can combine like terms on each side to obtain a single equation. What is the resulting equation in this case?

$\begin{align}
2x+3y &= -5 \\\\
\underline{\maroonD{+}
(5x-y)}&\phantom{=} \underline{\maroonD{+}\ (-12)} \\\\
\_\_\_\,x + \_\_\_\,y &= \_\_\_
\end{align}$

Adding the two equations gives us:

$7x+2y=-17$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the result of adding these two equations?**

$\begin{align}
-2x+7y &= -5 \\\\
-2x-4y &= 6
\end{align}$

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
When we $\maroonD{\text{add}}$ equations to each other, we can combine like terms on each side to obtain a single equation. What is the resulting equation in this case?

$\begin{align}
-2x+7y &= -5 \\\\
\underline{\maroonD{+} (-2x-4y)} &\phantom{=} \underline{\maroonD{+} (6)} \\\\
\_\_\_\,x + \_\_\_\,y &= \_\_\_
\end{align}$

Adding the two equations gives us:

$-4x +3y = 1$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the result of adding these two equations?**

$\begin{align}
5x-9y &= 1 \\\\
-7x+2y &= -5
\end{align}$

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
When we $\maroonD{\text{add}}$ equations to each other, we can combine like terms on each side to obtain a single equation. What is the resulting equation in this case?

$\begin{align}
5x-9y &= 1 \\\\
\underline{\maroonD{+} (-7x+2y)} &\phantom{=} \underline{\maroonD{+} (-5)} \\\\
\_\_\_\,x + \_\_\_\,y &= \_\_\_
\end{align}$

Adding the two equations gives us:

$-2x-7y=-4$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the result of adding these two equations?**

$\begin{align}
4x-4y &= -2 \\\\
-9x-4y &= -3
\end{align}$

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
When we $\maroonD{\text{add}}$ equations to each other, we can combine like terms on each side to obtain a single equation. What is the resulting equation in this case?

$\begin{align}
4x-4y &= -2 \\\\
\underline{\maroonD{+} (-9x-4y)} &\phantom{=} \underline{\maroonD{+} (-3)} \\\\
\_\_\_\,x + \_\_\_\,y &= \_\_\_
\end{align}$

Adding the two equations gives us:

$-5x -8y = -5$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the result of adding these two equations?**

$\begin{align}
5x-y &= 6 \\\\
-2x+y &= 8
\end{align}$

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
When we $\maroonD{\text{add}}$ equations to each other, we can combine like terms on each side to obtain a single equation. What is the resulting equation in this case?

$\begin{align}
5x-1y &= 6 \\\\
\underline{\maroonD{+} (-2x+1y)} &\phantom{=} \underline{\maroonD{+} (8)} \\\\
\_\_\_\,x + \_\_\_\,y &= \_\_\_
\end{align}$

Adding the two equations gives us:

$3x = 14$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the result of adding these two equations?**

$\begin{align}
-5x-9y &= 3 \\\\
5x-9y &= -2
\end{align}$

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
When we $\maroonD{\text{add}}$ equations to each other, we can combine like terms on each side to obtain a single equation. What is the resulting equation in this case?

$\begin{align}
-5x-9y &= 3 \\\\
\underline{\maroonD{+} (5x-9y)} &\phantom{=} \underline{\maroonD{+} (-2)} \\\\
\_\_\_\,x + \_\_\_\,y &= \_\_\_
\end{align}$

Adding the two equations gives us:

$ -18y = 1$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the result of adding these two equations?**

$\begin{align}
6x+2y &= -2 \\\\
3x-2y &= -5
\end{align}$

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
When we $\maroonD{\text{add}}$ equations to each other, we can combine like terms on each side to obtain a single equation. What is the resulting equation in this case?

$\begin{align}
6x+2y &= -2 \\\\
\underline{\maroonD{+} (3x-2y)} &\phantom{=} \underline{\maroonD{+} (-5)} \\\\
\_\_\_\,x + \_\_\_\,y &= \_\_\_
\end{align}$

Adding the two equations gives us:

$9x = -7$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the result of adding these two equations?**

$\begin{align}
2x+7y &= 4 \\\\
-2x-8y &= -2
\end{align}$

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
When we $\maroonD{\text{add}}$ equations to each other, we can combine like terms on each side to obtain a single equation. What is the resulting equation in this case?

$\begin{align}
2x+7y &= 4 \\\\
\underline{\maroonD{+} (-2x-8y)} &\phantom{=} \underline{\maroonD{+} (-2)} \\\\
\_\_\_\,x + \_\_\_\,y &= \_\_\_
\end{align}$

Adding the two equations gives us:

$ -y = 2$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the result of subtracting the second equation from the first?**

$\begin{align}
-2x+y &= 0 \\\\
-7x+3y &= 2
\end{align}$

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
When we $\maroonD{\text{subtract}}$ equations from each other, we can combine like terms on each side to obtain a single equation. What is the resulting equation in this case?

$\begin{align}
-2x+1y &= 0 \\\\
\underline{\maroonD{-} (-7x+3y)} &\phantom{=} \underline{\maroonD{-} (2)} \\\\
\_\_\_\,x + \_\_\_\,y &= \_\_\_
\end{align}$

Subtracting the two equations gives us:

$5x -2y = -2$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the result of subtracting the second equation from the first?**

$\begin{align}
8x-4y &= -4 \\\\
-3x+4y &= 5
\end{align}$

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
When we $\maroonD{\text{subtract}}$ equations from each other, we can combine like terms on each side to obtain a single equation. What is the resulting equation in this case?

$\begin{align}
8x-4y &= -4 \\\\
\underline{\maroonD{-} (-3x+4y)} &\phantom{=} \underline{\maroonD{-} (5)} \\\\
\_\_\_\,x + \_\_\_\,y &= \_\_\_
\end{align}$

Subtracting the two equations gives us:

$11x -8y = -9$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the result of subtracting the second equation from the first?**

$\begin{align}
x-3y &= 6 \\\\
-8x-y &= 6
\end{align}$

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
When we $\maroonD{\text{subtract}}$ equations from each other, we can combine like terms on each side to obtain a single equation. What is the resulting equation in this case?

$\begin{align}
1x-3y &= 6 \\\\
\underline{\maroonD{-} (-8x-1y)} &\phantom{=} \underline{\maroonD{-} (6)} \\\\
\_\_\_\,x + \_\_\_\,y &= \_\_\_
\end{align}$

Subtracting the two equations gives us:

$9x -2y = 0$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the result of subtracting the second equation from the first?**

$\begin{align}
-7x-y &= 0 \\\\
7x+8y &= -6
\end{align}$

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
When we $\maroonD{\text{subtract}}$ equations from each other, we can combine like terms on each side to obtain a single equation. What is the resulting equation in this case?

$\begin{align}
-7x-1y &= 0 \\\\
\underline{\maroonD{-} (7x+8y)} &\phantom{=} \underline{\maroonD{-} (-6)} \\\\
\_\_\_\,x + \_\_\_\,y &= \_\_\_
\end{align}$

Subtracting the two equations gives us:

$-14x -9y = 6$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the result of subtracting the second equation from the first?**

$\begin{align}
-2x+7y &= 10  \\\\
3x+7y &= 2
\end{align}$

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
When we $\maroonD{\text{subtract}}$ one equation from another, we can combine like terms on each side to obtain a single equation. What is the resulting equation in this case?

$\begin{align}
-2x+7y &= 10 \\\\
\underline{\maroonD{-}
(3x+7y)}&\phantom{=} \underline{\maroonD{-}\ (2)} \\\\
\_\_\_\,x + \_\_\_\,y &= \_\_\_
\end{align}$

Subtracting the second equation from the first gives us:

$-5x=8$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the result of subtracting the second equation from the first?**

$\begin{align}
-4x-2y &= -2 \\\\
x-2y &= 9
\end{align}$

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
When we $\maroonD{\text{subtract}}$ equations from each other, we can combine like terms on each side to obtain a single equation. What is the resulting equation in this case?

$\begin{align}
-4x-2y &= -2 \\\\
\underline{\maroonD{-} (1x-2y)} &\phantom{=} \underline{\maroonD{-} (9)} \\\\
\_\_\_\,x + \_\_\_\,y &= \_\_\_
\end{align}$

Subtracting the two equations gives us:

$-5x = -11$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the result of subtracting the second equation from the first?**

$\begin{align}
-3x+2y &= 7 \\\\
-3x-2y &= 4
\end{align}$

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
When we $\maroonD{\text{subtract}}$ equations from each other, we can combine like terms on each side to obtain a single equation. What is the resulting equation in this case?

$\begin{align}
-3x+2y &= 7 \\\\
\underline{\maroonD{-} (-3x-2y)} &\phantom{=} \underline{\maroonD{-} (4)} \\\\
\_\_\_\,x + \_\_\_\,y &= \_\_\_
\end{align}$

Subtracting the two equations gives us:

$4y = 3$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the result of subtracting the second equation from the first?**

$\begin{align}
2x+7y &= -8 \\\\
2x-5y &= -1
\end{align}$

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
When we $\maroonD{\text{subtract}}$ equations from each other, we can combine like terms on each side to obtain a single equation. What is the resulting equation in this case?

$\begin{align}
2x+7y &= -8 \\\\
\underline{\maroonD{-} (2x-5y)} &\phantom{=} \underline{\maroonD{-} (-1)} \\\\
\_\_\_\,x + \_\_\_\,y &= \_\_\_
\end{align}$

Subtracting the two equations gives us:

$12y = -7$
